notes: UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.

Students majoring in a different program of study may find that a minor in anthropology is an invaluable addition to their educational plan. Prospective transfer students interested in this minor are encouraged to complete three lower-division anthropology courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: ANTH 1 – Introduction to Biological Anthropology ANTH 2 – Introduction to Cultural Anthropology ANTH 3 – Introduction to Archaeology Refer to the “Preparation for the Minor” section below for courses approved to apply to each of the requirements listed above. Please visit the Anthropology website to learn more about this minor.
